perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any information or data, of any kind or nature whatsoever , relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to any trade secret, invention, idea, know -how, technical, financial and business information, business plans or processes , information provided by third parties to the Discloser under an obligation of confi dentiality , and all information which the Recipient has been exposed to in connection with the Subject Matter.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” means only that information or data which is explicitly marked as 'Confidential' in writing by the Discloser at the time of disclosure. This includes documents, emails, and any other tangible forms of communication. It does NOT include any information disclosed orally unless confirmed in writing as 'Confidential' within five (5) business days of the oral disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by narrowing the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only explicitly marked information. This contradicts the original definition in section 1.1, which includes all information exchanged between the Parties, regardless of whether it is explicitly marked. This creates uncertainty about what information is actually protected under the agreement, particularly in the earlier text where it states that all information exchanged is considered Confidential. This also introduces uncertainty and is thus a legal risk for both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any information or data, of any kind or nature whatsoever , relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to any trade secret, invention, idea, know -how, technical, financial and business information, business plans or processes , information provided by third parties to the Discloser under an obligation of confi dentiality , and all information which the Recipient has been exposed to in connection with the Subject Matter. For greater certainty, all information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information, unless specifically stated otherwise . “Confidential Information ” will also include this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include explicitly marked information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines 'Confidential Information' as any information exchanged between the parties, regardless of whether it is explicitly marked or not. This creates a strong and irreconcilable conflict, making it unclear what information is actually protected under the agreement and thus causing an in-text contradiction as per the definition of inconsistencies.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not use any Confidential Information for any purpose other th an the Subject Matter and will not use any Confidential Information for obtaining any intellectual property rights for the Recipient or to divert or attempt to divert any business, supplier or customer of the Discloser .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may use any Confidential Information for any purpose related or unrelated to the Subject Matter. The Recipient is also permitted to use Confidential Information for obtaining intellectual property rights, diverting business, suppliers, or customers of the Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating the Recipient may use Confidential Information for any purpose. This directly contradicts the preceding clause (2.3), which restricts the use of Confidential Information to only the Subject Matter and prohibits its use for obtaining intellectual property rights or diverting business. This complete reversal of the restriction causes in-text uncertainty over whether the Recipient is allowed to use the Discloser's Confidential Information for their own gain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will not use any Confidential Information for any purpose other th an the Subject Matter and will not use any Confidential Information for obtaining any intellectual property rights for the Recipient or to divert or attempt to divert any business, supplier or customer of the Discloser .
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Recipient may use Confidential Information for any purpose, including obtaining intellectual property rights and diverting business, suppliers, or customers of the Discloser. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which prohibits the Recipient from using Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Subject Matter and explicitly prohibits its use for obtaining intellectual property rights or diverting business. This is a direct and clear conflict, leading to in-text uncertainty and contradicting terms. The change aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient , will not be disclosed to any person or entity in any way except as provided in this Agreement , and will be protected with the same degree of care the Recipient normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient, will not be disclosed to any person or entity in any way except as provided in this Agreement; however, the Recipient is only required to exercise minimal effort to protect Confidential Information, and is not liable for unintentional disclosures.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by weakening the protection requirements for Confidential Information. While initially stating it will be maintained in confidence, the addition of 'however, the Recipient is only required to exercise minimal effort to protect Confidential Information, and is not liable for unintentional disclosures' directly contradicts the original intention that it must be protected with at least reasonable care. This ambiguity causes confusion regarding the degree of care required and creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the Recipient , will not be disclosed to any person or entity in any way except as provided in this Agreement , and will be protected with the same degree of care the Recipient normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating the Recipient is only required to exercise minimal effort and is not liable for unintentional disclosures, while the contradicted text (which is identical to the original text) requires the Recipient to protect Confidential Information with at least 'reasonable care'. Minimal effort is less than reasonable care, therefore, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text, creating uncertainty about the degree of care required.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent the Recipient is required to disclose any Confidential Information pursuant to a valid subpoena or other applicable order by a governmental agency or judicial body or by operation of law, the Recipient will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such disclosure so that the Discloser may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy from the proper authority.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent the Recipient is required to disclose any Confidential Information pursuant to a valid subpoena or other applicable order by a governmental agency or judicial body or by operation of law, the Recipient is not required to notify the Discloser of such disclosure, and has no obligation to cooperate with the Discloser in seeking a protective order.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation directly contradicts the original clause. Section 3 originally states that the Recipient will promptly notify the Discloser of any required disclosure of Confidential Information. This change explicitly removes the notification requirement and the obligation to cooperate in seeking a protective order. The new addition generates in-text ambiguity about the roles of the parties in the event of a legal mandate to disclose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To the extent the Recipient is required to disclose any Confidential Information pursuant to a valid subpoena or other applicable order by a governmental agency or judicial body or by operation of law, the Recipient will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such disclosure so that the Discloser may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy from the proper authority. The Recipient agrees to cooperate with the Discloser in seek ing such order or remedy. The Recipient further agrees that if the Recipient is required to disclose any Confidential Information , the Recipient will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required and will exercise all reasonable efforts to obtain reliable, written assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded to such Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text both state that the Recipient *will* notify the Discloser in the event of a subpoena. The changed text explicitly states the Recipient is *not* required to notify the Discloser. This creates a clear inconsistency about the Recipient's obligations, meeting the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction."

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees that, in the event of any breach of any provision hereof, the Discloser may, or will, not have an adequate remedy in money or damages. The Recipient therefore agrees that, in such event, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against such breach in any court of competent jurisdiction. Such injunctive relief will in no way limit the Discloser’s right to obtain other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees that, in the event of any breach of any provision hereof, the Discloser's sole and exclusive remedy shall be limited to monetary damages, not to exceed $100 CAD. The Discloser shall not be entitled to obtain injunctive relief.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by severely limiting the Discloser's remedies in case of a breach. The original section 5 states that the Discloser is entitled to injunctive relief. This change directly contradicts that by stating the Discloser's sole remedy is limited to $100 CAD in monetary damages and they cannot seek injunctive relief, resulting in ambiguity and uncertainty for the Discloser about the compensation in a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Equitable Relief . The Recipient agrees that, in the event of any breach of any provision hereof, the Discloser may, or will, not have an adequate remedy in money or damages. The Recipient therefore agrees that, in such event, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against such breach in any court of competent jurisdiction. Such injunctive relief will in no way limit the Discloser’s right to obtain other remedies available at law or in equity. No failure or delay by the Discloser hereto i n enforcing any right, power or privilege created hereunder shall operate as an implied waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial enforcement thereof preclude any other or further enforcement thereof or the enforcement of any other right, power or pr ivilege.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly limits the Discloser's remedies to monetary damages capped at $100 CAD and prohibits injunctive relief. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly states the Discloser is entitled to injunctive relief and that such relief doesn't limit other remedies. This creates a strong inconsistency regarding the Discloser's rights in case of a breach, therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will commence upon the Effective Date and terminate on the expiry or termination of any engagement in relation to the Subject Matter between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will commence upon the Effective Date and shall continue in perpetuity.
perturbation.explanation: This change causes an in-text contradiction by removing the termination clause tied to the Subject Matter and replacing it with a statement that the agreement continues in perpetuity. This is contradictory with section 10.1, which implies that the agreement ends with the engagement concerning the Subject Matter. Now, there is in-text uncertainty about the duration of the obligations under the Agreement due to contradictory termination terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will commence upon the Effective Date and terminate on the expiry or termination of any engagement in relation to the Subject Matter between the Parties. Upon termination or expiry of this Agreement, the Recipient (i) will cease to use the C onfidential Information received from the Discloser, (ii) within thirty (30) days after the date of termination or expiry of this Agreement, will return to the Discloser all original Confidential Information thereof then in its possession or control, (iii) if the Discloser requests in writing, within thirty (30) days after the date of termination or expiry of this Agreement, will return to the Discloser all copies of the Confidential Information requested by the Discloser, (iv) if no request from the Disclo ser has been received within thirty (30) days of the termination or expiry of this agreement for the return of any copies of the Confidential Information, will destroy all copies thereof then in its possession or control and (v) upon the written request o f the Discloser, will furnish the Discloser with written certification of such destruction .
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement terminates upon the expiry or termination of the engagement. The changed text states that the agreement continues in perpetuity. This directly contradicts the original termination clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which also states that the agreement terminates upon the expiry or termination of the engagement.
